title: LF10FIAE Skillset
subtitle: Design and develop user interfaces
intro: LF10a focuses on the structured development of user interfaces from requirements analysis to documentation.
The core topics are user-centered design, ergonomic implementation, testability, and maintainable realization.

subsections.title: Identify requirements for a user interface
items: Present the requirements-specification process in a structured way
Capture workflows and business processes in an as-is analysis
Create a requirements catalog with priorities
Separate functional and non-functional requirements
Document acceptance criteria early

subsections.title: Design the software product and classify its architecture
items: Place the user interface in the context of the overall architecture
Compare architectural patterns for the use case
Describe interfaces between UI and business logic cleanly
Justify design decisions in a traceable way
Align solution sketches with the team and the customer

subsections.title: Design user interfaces functionally and ergonomically
items: Distinguish GUI types and use cases
Apply ergonomics criteria to a UI systematically
Plan the design process with suitable tools
Choose GUI elements according to task and user group
Document evaluation criteria for user interfaces

subsections.title: Choose libraries and frameworks
items: Evaluate libraries against project requirements and team competence
Weigh framework risks such as lock-in and maintainability
Justify technology decisions with a criteria matrix
Check compatibility with the existing architecture
Include LTS and security aspects in the selection

subsections.title: Implement user interfaces in Python, Java, and the web
items: Create UI prototypes in Python for quick validation
Classify Java-based UIs for business desktop applications
Implement web-based interfaces for browser usage
Design input validation and error messages in a user-friendly way
Structure reusable UI building blocks

subsections.title: Test user interfaces and evaluate UX
items: Derive test cases for GUI function and interaction logic
Run usability tests with realistic scenarios
Prioritize findings and draw conclusions
Evaluate UX aspects in a measurable way
Plan follow-up tests to verify effectiveness

subsections.title: Document user interfaces
items: Create user documentation that fits the target audience
Record operating flows, prerequisites, and limits
Maintain versioning and change history
Deliver acceptance-ready documents for operations and support
Secure knowledge transfer for the team and end users
